selected_important_turn.text: Well since we were already given a few supplies before this deal, I was wondering if you could give me all 3 water, and I could give you all the firewood and 2 of the food packages. I only say all the water because we already have some and my medical condition sucks. lol I wish we could camp together! we seem to be a good match. lol! 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete allocation proposal from YOU and it stakes a maximal claim to your top item (all 3 waters). It strongly shapes the bargaining frame (zero-sum) and precedes the opponent's conciliatory counter-offer — so it heavily influences whether the negotiation moves toward cooperative trade or escalation.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The proposal is rigid (asking for all water) and framed as a demand despite earlier mutual disclosure. It risks appearing unfair/insensitive to the opponent's needs and could have preempted the integrative compromise the opponent later offered. Even with a legitimate medical need, asking for all of a scarce, highly-valued item is unnecessarily aggressive and reduces possibilities for mutually beneficial trade.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the claim and open for trade: instead of demanding all 3 waters, ask for the minimum you need in concrete terms (e.g., 2 waters), express appreciation for their needs, ask what their minimums are, and offer clearer compensating concessions (e.g., 2 food + 2 firewood for them). Frame it as a proposed trade rather than a take-it-all demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — more room to find integrative splits rather than locked zero-sum positions
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — perceived allocation will seem more balanced and considerate
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a softer, conditional request makes it easier for the opponent to accept or counteroffer
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — reduces risk of resentment and builds trust by showing willingness to trade rather than seize everything

value2reason.Low: I dont really need much of this because I can forage and find wood myself.
value2reason.Medium: You need food to survive but can live a long time without unlike water.
value2reason.High: Water is how you survive and I take a medication that requires a lot of water consumption

value2reason.Low: Water is something that is pretty plentiful naturally when out camping, so I'm hoping I could supplement with fresh spring water if need be.
value2reason.Medium: I need a decent amount of firewood both to keep warm, and to be able to cook. Maybe it's not AS important as the food, but still pretty important.
value2reason.High: I will only have the food I bring. I'm not the kind of person who could go "kill" my own food if I were to run out. I'm also not a forager, and would have no idea what was safe to eat besides what I brought.

dialogue.text: What is it that you really need on this camping trip?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I really need some more food. I only have the basics, and I'm afraid it's not going to be enough. What about yourself?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I really need water because I take medication that requires drinking a lot of water. The firewood I dont need to much off and I need very little food as I am dieting. lol 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I totally understand that. And it's pretty important, lol
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: It sure is..So how much food do you need for the trip? Do you need firewood to cook the food? Thank you for understanding my issues. 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I definitely need the food the most. I'm definitely not a "hunter", so if I were to run out, I'd be in pretty sad shape. I do need the firewood for both cooking, and keeping warm. It gets cold at night where we're going. How about youself?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well since we were already given a few supplies before this deal, I was wondering if you could give me all 3 water, and I could give you all the firewood and 2 of the food packages. I only say all the water because we already have some and my medical condition sucks. lol I wish we could camp together! we seem to be a good match. lol! 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm not sure I can agree to that. I really need the food and firewood more than water. How about if I take 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water, and you take the remaining 1 food, 1 firewood, and 2 water? It seems fairer to me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your offer, but I need more water for my medication. How about I take 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you take the remaining 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood? It’s a fair split that respects both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that split could work. Let me confirm: you take 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood, and I take 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that split works for me. Let's confirm: I take 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood, and you take 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood. Thank you for being understanding and working with me!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Agreed! Final split: I take 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood; you take 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood. Thank you for working together!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
